parentQuestion: Trauma to a nerve of the brachial plexus may cause wrist drop.

scaffolds.q: The radial nerve controls muscles that extend the wrist.
scaffolds.explain: Yes — and this is elegant. The radial nerve innervates ALL the extensor muscles of the forearm. These muscles extend (straighten) the wrist and fingers — they're the 'lift' muscles that fight gravity. you're about to see what happens when this fails.

scaffolds.q: If the muscles that lift the wrist are paralyzed, the wrist will hang limp.
scaffolds.explain: Absolutely — and This is the breathtaking bit:. Without functioning extensor muscles, gravity pulls the wrist down into a flexed position. This condition is called 'wrist drop' and indicates radial nerve injury. The wrist just dangles — no tone, no control, no function. Immerse yourself in this:that loss.
